source_name: Code4rena Kinetiq Findings and Analysis Report (2025-04)
finding_title: Buffer Silently Locks Staked HYPE in Contract Without Using Them For Withdrawals Or Providing A Way To Be Pulled Out Or Moved To L1
summary: When users stake into the Staking Manager and get their KHYPE tokens, after earning some rewards they might want to queue a withdrawal to get their HYPE tokens back. While the queued withdrawal delay is on, the user can decide to cancelWithdrawal and get their KHYPE tokens back. The way the buffer is handled in this flow leads to locking of HYPE in the staking manager contract.
root_cause: buffer accounting desync on cancelWithdrawal: hypeBuffer is deducted when queueing a withdrawal but not restored or bridged correctly when the withdrawal is cancelled
broken_invariants: HYPE removed from hypeBuffer to satisfy a queued withdrawal must remain usable for withdrawal liquidity or be restored on cancellation
cancelled withdrawals must not turn buffer-resident HYPE into untracked or incorrectly redelegated funds
redelegating cancelled withdrawal amounts must use the correct L1/EVM movement path for where the HYPE actually resides
attack_preconditions: Protocol state reaches the report-described edge case or queue configuration
Relevant manager/operator/user flow is executed as described in the public finding
attacker_capability: external user or protocol participant able to interact with the affected staking/withdrawal/deposit flow
trigger_actions: execute the affected function sequence described in the report
observe accounting/state transition divergence or operation failure

impact.description: When users stake into the Staking Manager and get their KHYPE tokens, after earning some rewards they might want to queue a withdrawal to get their HYPE tokens back. While the queued withdrawal delay is on, the user can decide to cancelWithdrawal and get their KHYPE tokens back. The way the buffer is handled in this flow leads to locking of HYPE in the staking manager contract.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and exploit/impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: check whether the affected function is actually reachable under current roles/pauses/configuration
check whether sponsor mitigations or operational assumptions fully eliminate the stated state transition
check whether the code version and line links match the audited commit
mitigations: Ensure that when the the canceled withdrawn amount is taken from the buffer, the buffer is either re-bumped or the assets are first moved to L1 to avoid being locked in the staking manager contract.
